title: Itanagar Wildlife Sanctuary
tagline: Itanagar Wildlife Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Arunachal Pradesh

overview.short: The Itanagar wildlife sanctuary was established in 1968 with the aim to conserve the biodiversity around the Itanagar sanctuary.
overview.description: The Itanagar wildlife sanctuary was established in 1968 with the aim to conserve the biodiversity around the Itanagar sanctuary. The reserve forest around Itanagar became part of the sanctuary. The sanctuary has geographical boundary limits as River Pam to the east, Pachin in the south, Neorochi on the north-East and the Chingke stream in the north. The terrain is mostly hilly.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Ganga Lake (India)
topPlaces.description: Gyekir Sinyi also known as Ganga Lake, is a lake in Itanagar, the capital of Arunachal Pradesh. It is a natural, freshwater lake that is located within the Itanagar Wildlife Sanctuary.

topPlaces.name: Itanagar
topPlaces.description: Itanagar () is the capital and largest town of the Indian state of Arunachal Pradesh. The seat of Arunachal Pradesh Legislative Assembly, the seat of government of Arunachal Pradesh, and the seat of Gauhati High Court permanent bench at Naharlagun are all in Itanagar.

topPlaces.name: Ita Fort
topPlaces.description: Ita Fort in Itanagar town is an important historical site in the state of Arunachal Pradesh, India. The name literally means "Fort of Bricks" (brick being called "Ita" in the Assamese language).

topPlaces.name: Diocese of Itanagar
topPlaces.description: The Roman Catholic Diocese of Itanagar (Dioecesis Itanagarensis) in India was created on 7 December 2005 by splitting it from the Diocese of Tezpur. It is a suffragan diocese of the Archdiocese of Guwahati.

topPlaces.name: Lok Bhavan, Itanagar
topPlaces.description: Lok Bhavan formerly Raj Bhavan (translation: Government House) is the official residence of the governor of Arunachal Pradesh. It is located in the capital city of Itanagar, Arunachal Pradesh.

topPlaces.name: Along West
topPlaces.description: Along West is a place in the Indian state of Arunachal Pradesh. West Siang is the name of the district that contains Tehsil Along West.

topPlaces.name: Sood, Arunachal Pradesh
topPlaces.description: Sood is a village near Naharlagun in Papum Pare district of Arunachal Pradesh

items.note: Gyekir Sinyi also known as Ganga Lake, is a lake in Itanagar, the capital of Arunachal Pradesh. It is a natural, freshwater lake that is located within the Itanagar Wildlife Sanctuary.

Itanagar () is the capital and largest town of the Indian state of Arunachal Pradesh. The seat of Arunachal Pradesh Legislative Assembly, the seat of government of Arunachal Pradesh, and the seat of Gauhati High Court permanent bench at Naharlagun are all in Itanagar.
Ita Fort in Itanagar town is an important historical site in the state of Arunachal Pradesh, India. The name literally means "Fort of Bricks" (brick being called "Ita" in the Assamese language).

The Roman Catholic Diocese of Itanagar (Dioecesis Itanagarensis) in India was created on 7 December 2005 by splitting it from the Diocese of Tezpur. It is a suffragan diocese of the Archdiocese of Guwahati.
Lok Bhavan formerly Raj Bhavan (translation: Government House) is the official residence of the governor of Arunachal Pradesh. It is located in the capital city of Itanagar, Arunachal Pradesh.
Along West is a place in the Indian state of Arunachal Pradesh. West Siang is the name of the district that contains Tehsil Along West.
Sood is a village near Naharlagun in Papum Pare district of Arunachal Pradesh

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Itanagar Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Itanagar Wildlife Sanctuary is Mar – Jun, Sep – Nov. Summers are 15–28°C and winters are 2–16°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Itanagar Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Jorhat Airport (70 km) and the nearest railway station is Dimapur Railway Station (130 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Itanagar Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Itanagar Wildlife Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Itanagar Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Itanagar Wildlife Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Itanagar Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Ganga Lake (India), Itanagar, Ita Fort, Diocese of Itanagar, Lok Bhavan, Itanagar.
